title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi with our whole slow roasted recipe. Perfect for special occasions, this dish is rich, flavorful, and a true culinary delight. Get the recipe!
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, garnished with nuts and herbs, ready to serve for a Middle Eastern feast.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of Middle Eastern cuisine with our show-stopping whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, perfect for any celebration.

description: Experience the grandeur of Middle Eastern hospitality with our whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi. This magnificent dish features tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb infused with aromatic spices, served atop a bed of fragrant rice. It is a celebratory centerpiece that promises an unforgettable culinary journey for your guests.

ingredients.notes: almonds, pistachios, pine nuts, toasted for garnish
cumin, coriander, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper, turmeric, allspice

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. Make deep incisions all over the lamb with a sharp knife. In a bowl, mix 3 tablespoons of Ouzi spice blend with 1/4 cup of ghee or olive oil, salt, and pepper. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the incisions. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or preferably overnight in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Prepare the Stuffing (Optional)
instructions.text: For the rice stuffing, heat 2 tablespoons of ghee in a large pot. Add chopped onions and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add minced garlic and ground lamb/beef, breaking it up with a spoon. Cook until browned. Stir in 1 tablespoon of Ouzi spice blend, salt, and pepper. Add the rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat. Pour in 4 cups of broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes until partially cooked. Fluff with a fork.

instructions.title: Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160C (325F). If stuffing, carefully fill the cavity of the lamb with the partially cooked rice mixture, securing the opening with butchers twine or skewers. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan. Pour 2 cups of broth into the bottom of the pan. Cover the pan tightly with foil.

instructions.title: Slow Roasting Process
instructions.text: Roast the lamb for 3-4 hours, or until very tender. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb. Baste the lamb with the pan juices every hour. For the last hour of cooking, remove the foil to allow the lamb skin to crisp up. Increase the oven temperature to 180C (350F) if needed for browning.

instructions.title: Prepare the Remaining Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb is finishing, prepare the remaining 4 cups of basmati rice. Heat 2 tablespoons of ghee in a separate pot. Add the rinsed rice, stir for a minute. Pour in 4 cups of broth (or water with a pinch of salt). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and rice is tender. Let it rest for 5 minutes before fluffing.

instructions.title: Serve the Ouzi
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the whole roasted lamb to a large serving platter. Arrange the fluffy basmati rice around the lamb. Garnish generously with toasted mixed nuts and fresh chopped parsley or coriander. Serve immediately with yogurt, a fresh salad, or a side of traditional Middle Eastern gravy made from the pan drippings.

tips: For maximum flavor, marinate the lamb overnight. The longer it marinates, the deeper the spice penetration and tenderness.
Do not overcrowd the roasting pan. Ensure there is enough space for air circulation for even cooking. If your lamb is very large, consider using two pans.
Always rest the lamb for at least 20-30 minutes after roasting before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and flavorful meat.
article: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a beloved Middle Eastern Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
